claims.summary: Trusted Rust constructors could be misused as persisted-input validators.
claims.disposition: Verified constructors are crate-private, trusted records do not implement Deserialize, and persisted JSON is handled by untrusted projector validation.

claims.summary: Receipt and source hashes need deterministic validation outside the Rust builder.
claims.disposition: Verified Python models validate bounded identifiers, SHA-256 values, deterministic IDs, exact keys, and evidence-only authority.

claims.summary: Presence context must not imply uptake.
claims.disposition: Verified ReciprocalContextReceiptV2 separates delivery/read/reply context from actor-authored uptake and contains no uptake inference.
